—

Theme

CRT dials shared crt.glsl · all tubes

The first nine dials are the constants in crt.glsl — one shader shared by every tube profile; screen padding is a per-profile Ghostty setting. The preview above runs the identical shader math, so what you see is what Ghostty renders.

Screen text

Publish back to Ghostty

Paste the publish command into your terminal — it writes retro/<slug> (and the shared crt.glsl if CRT is on) and opens a new window wearing this profile. A running window keeps the look it was launched with — pasting into one won't repaint it; ⌘⇧, only reloads the shared shader. Nothing here touches your files until you paste.

Publish command (paste in terminal)
Ghostty config — retro/<slug>
crt.glsl — shared by all tubes

Retro Terminals · Ghostty Studio — browse, tune, publish. Colors from build_profiles.py; shader from shaders/crt.glsl. Re-run build_ghostty.py --studio after editing the SPEC.
